_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national cron
_meta.note: Federal House batch: TN-07 D primary (David Jones, Joshua Sales), TN-09 R primary (James New), TX-21 placeholder RESOLVED -> Mark Teixeira (won 3/3 primary outright, GOP nominee), TX-22 Rebecca Clark CONFIRMED LOST. Evidence-first; nulls where no public position; currency corrections (TN-07 is NOT open — Van Epps R incumbent; Clark lost).

set.office: U.S. Representative TN-07 (2026 D primary candidate · challenger; seat held by R incumbent Matt Van Epps)

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-17: confirmed on the Aug 6, 2026 TN-07 Democratic primary ballot, but minimal-footprint candidate — no campaign website, no Ballotpedia Candidate Connection survey, no quoted statements, no endorsements found. No public policy positions to score; scored nothing rather than infer from party.

david-jones-tn-07.notes_set: David Jones — declared Democratic candidate in the Aug 6, 2026 TN-07 primary (general Nov 3, 2026). CURRENCY CORRECTION: TN-07 is NOT an open seat — Republican Matt Van Epps won the Dec 2, 2025 special election (53.9% to Aftyn Behn 45.1%) after Mark Green's July 2025 resignation and is the 2026 incumbent; TN-07 was also redrawn (map signed 2026-05-07). Minimal-footprint candidate: no campaign site, no candidate survey, no policy statements or endorsements located as of 2026-06-17, so all rubric cells are null (no fabrication from party). Re-check after the Aug 6, 2026 primary.

set.office: U.S. Representative TN-07 (2026 D primary candidate · challenger; seat held by R incumbent Matt Van Epps)

profile.background: Joshua Warren Sales — AP English teacher (Davidson County); U.S. Army veteran (Explosive Ordnance Disposal); Chair, Tennessee High School Democrats; resident of Pegram/Cheatham County, TN.
profile.confidence_note: Scored from his campaign-site platform (sales4congress.com). Issue positions are economic/infrastructure-focused; he explicitly marks social-issue positions 'in development', so sanctity/marriage/education/religious-liberty/guns cells are null (no statement), not inferred.

joshua-sales.notes_set: Joshua Warren Sales — declared Democratic candidate in the Aug 6, 2026 TN-07 primary (one of several Democrats: Copeland, Dixie, Holloway). CURRENCY: TN-07 is NOT open — R incumbent Matt Van Epps (won Dec 2025 special). Documented platform (sales4congress.com): backs the Sanders-Grassley 'Audit the Pentagon Act' (-> Pentagon-audit/defense-accountability TRUE); proposes a pro-immigrant 'Welcoming the Stranger Act' (-> contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens, FALSE); also supports universal healthcare, a wealth tax, rural infrastructure, anti-interventionist/reduced-war-spending framing. Social-issue cells null — platform marks them 'in development'; no abortion/marriage/2A/election statement or single-issue endorsement found.

4.note: Campaign platform: backs the Sanders-Grassley 'Audit the Pentagon Act' — supports completion of Pentagon audits / defense-contractor accountability.

1.note: Platform proposes a 'Welcoming the Stranger Act' (pro-immigrant reform) — documented contrary to mandatory deportation of all illegal aliens.

set.office: U.S. Representative TN-09 (2026 R primary candidate · challenger; redrawn district)

profile.confidence_note: Evidence sweep 2026-06-17: confirmed FEC-registered Republican challenger (candidate ID H6TN09456, filed 2025-11-16, status active), but has raised no funds, has no principal campaign committee, no campaign website or social media, and no public policy statements of any kind. No positions to score; scored nothing rather than infer from party.

james-new-tn-09.notes_set: James New — FEC-registered Republican challenger for TN-09 (candidate ID H6TN09456, filed 2025-11-16; address Munford/Tipton Co., TN), listed in the Aug 6, 2026 GOP primary field (with Charlotte Bergmann, Kevin Young). FEC shows no funds raised and no committee; no campaign website, social media, or policy statements located as of 2026-06-17. TN-09 is a redrawn district for 2026 (map signed 2026-05-07). All rubric cells null — no fabrication from party. (TN SoS qualified-candidate PDF returned 403; ballot-qualification not independently re-confirmed.)

set.name: Mark Teixeira
set.office: U.S. Representative TX-21 (2026 R nominee · open seat; won 3/3 primary outright)

profile.background: Mark 'Tex' Teixeira — former MLB All-Star; 2026 Republican nominee for TX-21 (open seat, north San Antonio / Austin suburbs / Hill Country) after winning the March 3, 2026 primary outright (~61%) in a 13-candidate field.
profile.confidence_note: Scored from campaign-site issue planks + Texas Tribune/The Hill coverage. Border, election-integrity, and trans-in-sports positions are HARD; abortion is a SOFT self-identification (no detailed plank or SBA endorsement). Cells without a clear citable statement (guns specifics, foreign policy, economy, industry) are null.

tx-21-r-placeholder.notes_set: Mark 'Tex' Teixeira — 2026 Republican NOMINEE for TX-21, an OPEN seat (incumbent Chip Roy vacated to run for TX Attorney General). Won the March 3, 2026 GOP primary OUTRIGHT (~61%, no runoff) in a 13-candidate field; advances to the Nov 3, 2026 general vs. Kristin Hook (D). [Record converted from the former 'tx-21-r-placeholder' now that the race has resolved.] Documented positions: 'secure the border, deport those here illegally, crush cartels' (National Border Patrol Council endorsement); 'secure elections' + proof-of-citizenship to vote; ban 'biological men' from women's sports; self-identifies pro-life (no detailed plank). Endorsed by Trump, Gov. Abbott, Speaker Mike Johnson, Jim Jordan, Scalise, Club for Growth, Moms for America Action, True Texas Project. Social-conservative planks (abortion specifics, marriage) are thin/SOFT — America-First border/economy profile.

0.note: Self-identifies pro-life (SOFT — no detailed plank or SBA Pro-Life endorsement found).

2.note: Campaigns on banning 'biological men' from women's sports — affirms biological sex / rejects transgender ideology.

1.note: Campaigns on 'secure elections' and proof-of-citizenship to vote.
Core plank: 'deport those here illegally'; endorsed by the National Border Patrol Council.

set.office: U.S. Representative TX-22 (2026 R primary candidate · LOST to Trever Nehls 24%-76%, 2026-03-03)

profile.background: Rebecca Clark — exploration geophysicist (B.S. Geophysics, Texas A&M); Fort Bend County GOP precinct chair, election judge, Executive Director of Fort Bend County Citizens Defending Freedom; served on Lamar CISD curriculum-review and bond-oversight committees.
profile.confidence_note: CURRENCY WIN: confirmed LOST the March 3, 2026 TX-22 Republican primary to Trever Nehls (23.9% to 76.1%, one-on-one, no runoff). Marked status: lost — leaves the active pool. Stated positions are slogan-level ('Faith. Family. Freedom.'); only her own-words pro-life and pro-2A self-identification are individually attributable. Issue cells null where no specific citable statement; positions documented in notes.

rebecca-clark-tx-22.notes_set: Rebecca Clark — LOST the March 3, 2026 TX-22 Republican primary to Trever Nehls (retired Army colonel, twin of outgoing Rep. Troy Nehls, Trump-endorsed): Clark 23.9% vs Nehls 76.1%, no runoff. TX-22 is an open seat (Troy Nehls retiring); Trever Nehls is the GOP nominee for the Nov 3, 2026 general. Clark is OUT. Documented self-framing (rebeccaclark4congress.com, 'Faith. Family. Freedom.'): pro-life ('defend... life'), pro-Second Amendment, 'student-focused, parent-guided education', 'secure border / consistent enforcement', fiscal conservatism. Positions are slogan-level — no detailed planks, no single-issue endorsements found; Trump endorsed her opponent. Rubric cells null (slogans do not meet the specific-cell bar); the verified primary loss is the deliverable.
